perturbation.original_text: 3. Confidentiality obligations 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential i nformation of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care; and [(c) that it will act in good faith at all times in relation to the Confidential Information, and will not use any of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose.]
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Confidentiality obligations 3.1 The Recipient agrees and undertakes: (a) that it will keep all Confidential Information strictly confidential and will not disclose any part of it to any o ther person without the Disclose r's prior written consent[, and then only under conditions of confidentiality no less onerous than those contained in this Agreement]; (b) that it will use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential i nformation of a similar nature, being at least a reasonable degree of care; and
perturbation.explanation: By removing clause (c) which states '[...] will not use any of the Confidential Information for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose,'] it creates an in-text contradiction since it removes a key condition that restricts the recipient's use of confidential information and makes enforcement of the confidentiality obligations of the agreement much more difficult. If there is no definition of what is and isn't accepted as 'Permitted Purpose'. The recipient could argue that any use of the Confidential Information is acceptable because there is no restriction and the contract becomes unenforcable. Without this clause, the core principle of the NDA becomes undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement: “Agreement ” means this non disclosure a greement and any amendments to it from time to time; “Confidential Information ” means: [(a) any information disclosed b y [or on behalf of] the Disclose r to the Recipient [during the Term / before the end of the Term] (whether disclosed in writing, orally or otherwise) that at the time of disclosure: (i) was marked as “confidential”; or (ii) should have been reasonably understood by the Recipient to be confidential;] [(b) the terms [(but not the existence)] of this Agreement; and]5 [(c) [specify other confidential information here ];] “Permitted Purpose ” means input with respect to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines “ TM “ and “Term ” means the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 3.1(c) explicitly restricts the use of confidential information to the 'Permitted Purpose'. The changed text omits this restriction, directly contradicting the definition of 'Permitted Purpose' provided in the 'Contradicted Text'. Without the clause, the recipient could argue that any use is acceptable as explained, rendering the definition of 'Permitted Purpose' and the confidentiality obligations themselves unenforceable. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the contract now lacks a clear limitation on how the Confidential Information can be used, directly conflicting with the original intent and the definition of 'Permitted Purpose'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement [forthwith] at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 [Either party] may termina te this Agreement at any time by giving written notice of termination to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'forthwith', the meaning of the agreement is changed. 'Forthwith' indicates immediate termination upon written notice. Without this term, the recipient has wiggle room to take action before the contract is 'forthwith' terminated. Therefore, it creates ambiguity regarding the effective termination date, specifically contradicting the subsequent clauses in Section 5.2 that describe the obligations upon termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.2 Upon termination: [(a) the Recipient will immediately cease to use the Confidential Information; and] (b) all the provisions of this Agreement will cease to have effect, save that the following provisions of this Agreement will survive and continue to have effect (in accordance with their terms or otherwise indefinitely): Clauses [1, 3, 5.2 to 5.4, and 6].
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'forthwith' in Section 5.1, as explained, creates ambiguity regarding the termination date. The contradicted text, Section 5.2, specifies immediate actions upon termination. The absence of 'forthwith' contradicts the expectation of immediacy detailed in 5.2, because without 'forthwith', it is no longer immediately, and the Recipient will cease immediately. This directly impacts the obligations of the recipient described in 5.2, making the timing unclear and creating a direct conflict, triggering the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.3 Within [5] working days following the date of effective termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.]
perturbation.changed_text: 5.3 Following the date of termination of this Agreement the Recipient will d estroy or return to the Disclose r (at the Disclose r's option) all media containing Confidential Information, and will irrevocably delete and remove all Confidential Information from its co mputer systems.]
perturbation.explanation: By removing '[5] working days', it removes the timeframe in which the recipient is obligated to dispose of the confidential information. This creates an in-text contradiction because it doesn't specify the time frame in which it needs to be completed. By removing the timeframe, it creates ambiguity on when the contract is technically considered fulfilled.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4 Termination of this Agreement will not affect either party’s accrued rights as at the date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 5.3 specifies a timeframe of '[5] working days' for the recipient to dispose of confidential information. This timeframe is removed in the changed text, creating ambiguity about when the obligation must be fulfilled. The contradicted text in 5.4 states that termination will not affect either party's accrued rights as of the termination date. By removing the timeframe, it is unclear how long after the termination date the recipient has to dispose of the information, which can affect accrued rights that could arise if the information is not handled as specified. Thus, removing the timeframe causes an in-text contradiction.

If you plan to change the governing law, you should have the document reviewed by someone with expertise in the law of the relevant jurisdiction. 10 As a practical matter, it makes sense for the courts with expertise in the relevan t law to have the right to adjudicate disputes. Purpose being input with respect to input with respect to Urban Wind Turbines ™ The parties have indicated their acceptance of this Agreement by executing it below.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.7 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [England and Wales]; and the courts of [England] will have exclusive jurisdiction to The parties have indicated their acceptance of this Agreement by executing it below.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the disclaimer about needing an expert to change governing law, it creates an in-text contradiction. The text implies the agreement is meant to be explicitly under English Law, but there isn't legal text stating there needs to be an expert in law, and can be changed without proper oversight, creating a large legal risk. Therefore the implication of its statement of being governed by English law, does not ensure the process of maintaining the integrity of the law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.7 This Agreement will be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of [England and Wales]; and the courts of [England] will have exclusive jurisdiction to The parties have indicated their acceptance of this Agreement by executing it below.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes a disclaimer about seeking expert legal advice when changing the governing law. The changed text removes this disclaimer, creating a contradiction because it implies that the agreement is explicitly under English law. However, the lack of a requirement for expert oversight when changing the law introduces legal risk, directly conflicting with the implication of maintaining the integrity of English law. The contradicted text also states that the agreement is governed by English law, which is explicitly contradicted by removing the disclaimer.
